summary: Blazing Scarlet>Fire, soaring Sugaree and a tight second‑set groove
blurb: The show delivers a blistering Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain sequence and an electrifying Sugaree. Jerry’s guitar is on fire while the rhythm section drives a powerful second set featuring Samson & Delilah, a soaring Wharf Rat solo and seamless UJB transitions. Overall performance ranks among the strongest of the spring ’77 run.
review: May 11, 1977 at the St. Paul Civic Center captures the Dead in peak form. The first set builds momentum with crisp renditions of Big River, Loser, Looks Like Rain and a standout Lazy Lightning → Supplication jam that sets the tone for the night. The second half erupts with a blazing Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue, a muscular Samson & Delilah (highlighted by Keith’s piano work), and an inspired Wharf Rat solo that flows into a joyous encore of Brokedown Palace. Jerry Garcia is repeatedly praised for scorching guitar tones and heartfelt vocals, delivering blistering solos on Scarlet, Sugaree and throughout the set. Phil Lesh lays down a solid bass foundation, while Bob Weir adds reliable rhythm chops and tight harmonies. The dual‑drum attack from Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann underpins the high‑energy flow, and Keith Godchaux’s keyboards provide tasteful texture on Looks Like Rain and Samson. Across multiple soundboard sources the recording quality is excellent—crisp, well‑balanced and free of major glitches—making this night a must‑listen for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: The majority of recordings (5 out of 6) are marked positive, repeatedly praising the band's locked‑in playing, blazing guitar work, tight rhythm section and standout songs such as Scarlet>Fire, Sugaree and Lazy Lightning > Supplication. Only one recording notes a few rough spots, which does not outweigh the overwhelming acclaim. This consistent high praise yields a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Pristine soundboard capture with good mix, clear instrument separation and no distracting flaws; it showcases the band's performance at its best.

key_highlights: Smoking Sugaree that rivals the famed Ithaca version
Blazing Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain sequence
Tight Lazy Lightning > Supplication jam in the first set
Powerful second‑set flow with Samson & Delilah, Wharf Rat solo and seamless UJB transition

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivered scorching guitar tones and heartfelt vocals, blazing solos on Scarlet Begonias, Sugaree and throughout the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provided a solid, driving bass foundation, delivering several tremendous moments that locked in with Jerry’s leads.
band_performance.Bob: Weir supplied reliable rhythm work and strong vocal harmonies, especially on Peggy-O and Big River.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart added tight double‑time grooves that propelled the high‑energy flow of the set.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann’s steady backbeat kept the pulse firm, contributing to the powerful second‑set swing.
